distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors map to named eval-planning misconceptions: metric mismatched to error cost, accuracy on imbalanced classes, tiny/minimized test set, CI-formula-required (deferred concept rejected), non-held-out / easiest-cases, calc-order, goalpost-moving, always-maximize, universal 50% threshold.

objective_coverage.note: All corpus §3 LOs covered: choose-metric-by-error-cost (q1,q2), justify-in-one-sentence (q1/q6 because-framing), trustworthy-test-set-size (q3) + held-out (q4), threshold-in-advance (q5), threshold-tied-to-stakes (q6), assemble-one-page-plan (q7).

short_answer.question: q7
short_answer.note: Valid GIFT short-answer: only = lines, no distractors. All accepted answers name the three decisions in order (metric, test-set size, threshold).

beginner_fairness_scope.note: DEEP profile, 7 questions (6 MCQ + 1 short-answer) — within 5-7. On-headline: no CI/Z-score math, no K-fold, no train/val/test-split methodology in correct paths; deferred concepts (CI formula at q3) appear only as a distractor a beginner rejects. Exactly one correct per MCQ; valid GIFT; clean stems (no stem-level #).

verdict: Pass — positional (max_share 0.333), length, distractor-plausibility, LO-coverage, short-answer, and on-headline scope all clean.
